title: Increase subuid/subgid to 65536
body:
#### What type of PR is this?

/kind other

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

We have a limit of 50000 for subuid and subgid in the buildah container image
for the build user which may be too low for when running rootless

Increasing the value to 65536 which should be good enough for
most cases.

#### How to verify it

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

/cc @nalind 

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: Thanks for this!  There are a couple of other Dockerfiles under contrib/buildahimage (the "centos7", "testing", and "upstream" directories) that also currently use a range size of 50000.  Any chance you could hit those as well, so that they all change together in a single PR?

This will prevent rootless users from being able to run the buildah container.  That is why 50000 was selected.

You can not a container image within a container image with more UIDs then provided by default.

Please attempt to do

$ podman run --user build buildah ...

And make sure it works, before changing this.

> This will prevent rootless users from being able to run the buildah container. That is why 50000 was selected.
> 
> You can not a container image within a container image with more UIDs then provided by default.
> 
> Please attempt to do
> 
> $ podman run --user build buildah ...
> 
> And make sure it works, before changing this.

we could use the range `1:65536`.  I've not tried, but I think it would work with rootless as well
Fine could you try that and see if it works.

sorry for the delay updating,

same error with 1:65536 

The only range id that would work would be starting from 2000 to 6336 as :

```
build:2000:63336
```

and it would build (i guess the error is because of my test environemnt) :

(i'll update the dockerfiles as well when we figure out which ranges is best)

1:65536 Should be 1:65535
See if that works.

Actually I think you might have to do something like:

build 1:999
build 1001:64535

That avoids the UID==1000 of the build user running the container.

ah that make sense, i didn't realized how this uid skipping worked.

this worked now :

```
